privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains how Mayra Jewelry ("we", "us") handles personal information when you sign in to our website using Facebook.
privacy.collectBody: When you sign in with Facebook, we receive your Facebook user ID, public name, and profile picture. If you place an order, we also collect the contact and shipping details you provide directly (name, phone, address).
privacy.useBody: We use this information to identify your account on our site, display your name and avatar in the interface, save your wishlist, and process orders you make. We do not sell your data and we do not use it for advertising.
privacy.shareBody: We share information only with service providers we need to operate the shop: Facebook (for sign-in), our hosting providers, our payment processor (for order verification), and our email provider (for transactional emails). We do not share your data for marketing purposes.
privacy.retentionBody: We keep your account data while your account is active. You can request deletion at any time — see the Delete Your Data page for instructions. After deletion, copies may persist in encrypted backups for up to 30 days before being purged.
privacy.rightsBody: You can ask us to access, correct, or delete the information we hold about you. You can also revoke our access at any time via your Facebook settings (Settings → Apps and Websites → Mayra → Remove).
privacy.contactBody: For privacy questions or data requests, contact us through our Facebook page or via email.

delete.title: Delete Your Data
delete.intro: You can request deletion of all data Mayra Jewelry holds about you at any time.
delete.storedBody: Your Facebook user ID, name, and profile picture (from Facebook sign-in), plus any order details, wishlist items, and contact information you have provided.
delete.howStep1: Send a message to our Facebook page or email us, with the subject "Delete my data". Include the name on the account.
delete.howStep2: Alternatively, remove the Mayra app from your Facebook settings: Settings → Apps and Websites → Mayra → Remove. This revokes our access immediately; we will then delete the associated record on our side.
delete.timelineBody: We will confirm your request within 7 days and complete deletion within 30 days. Encrypted backups containing the data are purged on a rolling 30-day cycle.
delete.contactBody: For questions about deletion or to follow up on a request, contact us through our Facebook page or via email.

sizeGuide.title: Find the size that feels right
sizeGuide.lead: A well-fitting piece quietly disappears on the body. Use these methods and reference tables to find the size that lets a Mayra piece sit the way it should — every day.

ring.title: Three ways to measure your ring size
ring.description: For the most accurate result, measure at the end of the day when fingers are slightly larger, and use the dominant hand if the ring is for everyday wear.

existing.title: Measure a ring you already own
steps: Pick a ring you already wear comfortably on the right finger.
Lay it flat and measure the inner diameter in millimetres, edge to edge.
Match the value to the reference table below.

string.title: String or paper method
steps: Wrap a thin strip of paper or string around the base of the finger.
Mark where the strip overlaps and measure the length in millimetres.
That length is the circumference — use the reference table to find your size.

visit.title: Message us for a sizer
steps: Send a message via our Facebook page and request a printable sizer or in-store fitting.
We'll guide you through the measurement step by step.
Sizing service is free and there's no purchase required.

ring.tableNote: Inner diameter is the most reliable measurement. Round up if you fall between two sizes.

bracelet.title: Sizing your wrist
bracelet.description: Bracelet sizing is more forgiving than rings. Choose snug for delicate chains, looser for bangles. The reference below assumes a comfortable everyday fit.

wrap.title: Wrap method
steps: Wrap a soft tape measure around the wrist, just above the wrist bone.
Keep it snug but not tight — this is your wrist circumference.
Add 1–2 cm depending on how loose you'd like the bracelet to sit.

existing.title: Measure a bracelet you wear
existing.best: If you have one you love
steps: Pick a bracelet that fits the way you'd like a new one to fit.
Lay it flat and measure end to end (excluding the clasp).
Match the length in centimetres to the reference table.

bracelet.tableNote: Sizes assume a comfortable everyday fit. Add up to 1 cm for a looser drape.

tips.title: A few quiet tips
items: Measure at the end of the day — fingers and wrists are slightly larger than in the morning.
Avoid measuring when hands are cold; the size you get will run small.
Knuckles wider than the base? Size for the knuckle and the ring will settle naturally.
If you're between two sizes, choose the larger one for everyday wear.
Ring widths over 4 mm tend to feel tighter — consider sizing up by half a step.
Still unsure? Message us on Facebook with a photo of your measurement and we'll help.
